events.title: Future Tech Leaders Summer Camp: AI, Cybersecurity, Engineering & Business
events.description: Students will get hands-on experience exploring some of the fastest-growing tech careers out there.

events.title: Pitch Please #9
events.description: Five early-stage AI founders selected from the AI2 Incubator applicant pool will pitch to a panel of experienced investors and incubator leaders—including AI2 Incubator Managing Director Yifan Zhang and CTO Pierre Martin—with the audience vote winner securing a guaranteed meeting. The monthly 2.5-hour session combines founder pitches, expert feedback, and networking with potential co-founders and fellow AI builders.

events.title: Moms Who Build: Maternal Health Hackathon — An AInChai + Armoire Meetup
events.description: Moms will tackle real unsolved problems from maternal health organizations in a high-energy 60-minute AI build sprint, creating actual solutions alongside builders and community members. With on-site childcare, honest conversations about building while parenting, and free AI tools provided, this is where the people closest to the problem become the ones solving it.

events.title: AI Tools to Build Your Business Idea Without Code
events.description: Kelly Kirk, founder of Kelly Tutors and educator to over 10,000 students, will walk you through a hands-on crash course covering AI research tools, web/app building, automation, and creative generation—you'll actually build something real in the 90 minutes. It's a mix of demos, guided discussion, and time to get your hands dirty with the tools yourself.
events.venue: Capitol Hill Branch - The Seattle Public Library

events.title: AI Specialists: AppliedAI Ethics Mixer – Chart Your Future
events.description: Specialists in applied AI will gather to network and exchange insights on developments in the field, bringing together a mix of practitioners and thought leaders working at the intersection of artificial intelligence and real-world applications.

events.title: AI Tech Founders, Investors & Pitch Night by AstraTech I
events.description: A dynamic gathering tailored for AI and tech founders, investors, and industry professionals will offer a prime opportunity to connect and explore cutting-edge innovations.

events.title: Aeone CreatorsXCreators Panel and Mixer
events.description: Creators across all tiers—from nano to mega—will gather for an exclusive panel featuring YouTubers with millions of followers like HipyoTech (1.7M), Noah Kane (725K), and Ryan Mitchell (368K+) discussing AI, audience growth, and sustainable creator businesses, complete with rooftop bites, a live DJ, and genuine networking among pre-screened creators only.

events.title: AI Governance in Education (Seattle + Online)
events.description: Education leaders and EdTech practitioners will hash out the real challenges of governing AI in schools—from protecting minors' data under FERPA and COPPA to surfacing shadow AI use and defending academic integrity—with three experienced speakers showing what they're actually building and shipping right now. The AiGovOps Foundation is hosting this practitioner-focused meetup where district CIOs, compliance officers, and anyone managing AI risk in learning environments can hear directly from the people in the trenches on what's working and where education AI governance is heading next.

events.title: Coding, Algorithms & AI
events.description: PyData Seattle's Coding, Algorithms & AI meetup will bring together software engineers, data scientists, researchers, and tech enthusiasts to explore cutting-edge developments in algorithms and AI. Attendees will dive into hands-on coding discussions and insights from professionals across the tech industry.

events.title: Beyond AI Models: Monetization, Security & Responsible Systems at Scale
events.description: Leaders shaping the future of AI monetization, security, and responsible systems at top global enterprises will share insights in a high-impact evening.

events.title: Where is AI in 2026 and Where is it Going?
events.description: A free, public workshop will explore the rapid advancements in AI systems, welcoming everyone regardless of technical background.

events.title: #20. AI/ML Code And Learn
events.description: Folks will be diving into AI/ML skills every other week, tackling a rotating lineup of topics designed to level up their technical chops.

events.title: AI Specialists: AIConnect - Your Gateway to AI Career Growth & Collaboration
events.description: AI specialists and tech professionals will gather to network and explore the latest in artificial intelligence technology.

events.title: Superintelligent AI and Loss of Control
events.description: A free public workshop will explore what genuine machine superintelligence might look like beyond specific tasks like chess or writing, diving into the real implications of AI systems potentially surpassing human capabilities.

events.title: AI + Vinyl Party
events.description: An AI Pop-Up vinyl listening session will explore digital burnout and attention in the age of AI, bringing together thoughtful conversation about how technology shapes our daily lives. Guests can expect deep dives into smartphone habits and AI's role in modern attention spans.

events.title: Dell x NVIDIA Hackathon: Local AI on Dell Pro Max with GB10
events.description: Teams of 2–4 will have one day to build and ship a locally-run AI business agent on real Dell Pro Max with GB10 hardware, competing against 39 other teams with a chance to take home the winning device. Top finishers will pitch live to judges that same evening, with first place winning a Dell Pro Max per team member.

events.title: AI Dev Tools Track
events.description: AI developers and architects will gather to demo cutting-edge dev tooling, debate AI stacks, and learn from folks at Amazon, Microsoft, Meta, and startups like Anthropic—featuring rapid-fire 5-minute demos with live code audits and real practical learnings around building AI systems. Expect discussions on adversarial reviews, Haskell optimization, and agentic automation alongside food and a room full of CTOs, founders, and principal architects who actually ship production AI.

events.title: AI Tinkerers Dubai - June Demo Day
events.description: AI Tinkerers: Dev Tools will bring together AI-first developers for dynamic demos, live code reviews, and fast-paced debates on cutting-edge AI development tools, fostering a collaborative environment for tech-savvy innovators.

events.title: AI House Founder Mental Health Affinity Group Meetup - Loneliness at the Top
events.description: Tech product founders will explore imposter syndrome in elite talent ecosystems through a facilitated, confidential peer discussion led by executive coach Brett Greene, PCC, focusing on emotional resilience and founder well-being.

events.title: GTM Strategy for Founders: A Fireside Chat with Kelly Breslin Wright
events.description: Kelly Breslin Wright, who scaled Tableau's sales from zero to $850M before its $15.7B Salesforce acquisition and later served as President and COO of Gong, will share insights on building repeatable go-to-market motions that compete at scale. This fireside chat will cover how founders can earn early customers and build sales organizations that survive growth.

events.title: New Tech Seattle July 2026 Meetup
events.description: New Tech Seattle’s July meetup will bring together 60,000+ tech professionals including developers, founders, investors, and executives for a night of networking, innovative 8-minute tech presentations, and lively community interactions, all fueled by career-relevant insights and industry expertise.

events.title: Women in AI - Builders Track: Seattle Inaugural Meetup
events.description: Women in AI - Builders Track will spotlight hands-on AI development with lightning demos, live code audits, and rapid-fire stack debates, geared toward practical engineering challenges and featuring expert AI builders.

events.title: AI in Action: Using Technology to Grow Business and Support Seniors
events.description: Get the lowdown on how AI will transform business operations and improve quality of life for seniors through hands-on exploration and real-world applications.

events.title: AI Specialists: AgenticAI Breakthroughs & Builder Mixer
events.description: Specialists in agentic AI will gather to network and exchange ideas on July 15th at 5 PM PDT, bringing together cutting-edge practitioners in the field.

events.title: AI Meetup - Post Alley
events.description: Seattle developers will dive into practical AI applications and experiments through live demos, connecting with fellow builders and creators who are actively shipping AI-powered tools and projects.

events.title: Don't just dream it, Build it
events.description: Senior product, design, engineering, and AI leaders will gather to dig into why teams are moving faster with AI tools but still hitting coordination bottlenecks—all while customizing their own Nike sneakers over drinks and conversation. This will be a candid exploration of what's actually working in the coordination layer that AI hasn't solved yet.

events.title: Outset Show & Tell
events.description: Researchers from Seattle will showcase AI-moderated research projects through Outset's platform, with presentations spanning topics from user friction and AI collaboration to inclusive design and trust-building—all while exploring how AI moderation is reshaping the research process. The evening kicks off with networking drinks followed by insights from seasoned practitioners on leveraging AI as a research tool.

events.title: July 15 - AI, ML and Computer Vision Meetup
events.description: This upcoming in-person meetup will feature expert talks on the latest advancements in AI, machine learning, and computer vision, offering insights from professionals deeply versed in these fields.

events.title: State of Climate Tech VC in the PNW 2026 Round Table - Live Podcast Recording
events.description: Five veteran climate tech investors—including the co-host of Climate Money podcast and founders from Toba Capital, Stepchange Ventures, SNØCAP, Elemental Impact, and Burnt Island Ventures—will dig into an unscripted conversation about where climate capital is actually flowing in 2026, what's getting funded, and whether AI and climate tech are really converging. Each investor brings their own burning question to this candid roundtable discussion facilitated by a Foster School of Business professor.

events.title: AI, Robotics, and the Future of Sustainable Agriculture
events.description: A panel will explore how AI and robotics are transforming sustainable agriculture, with speakers from Carbon Robotics, Voyager Capital, and UW researchers diving into real-world deployments, practical solutions, and the barriers holding back wider adoption. The conversation will span everything from cost and infrastructure challenges to environmental impact and what farmers actually need.

events.title: Shipping Real Software in the AI Era, with Francois Ajenstat, Andrew Beers, and Mark Nelson
events.description: Golden Analytics' CEO Francois Ajenstat and CTO Andrew Beers will dig into the unglamorous work behind production-grade AI—security, scalability, compliance—drawing from their battle-tested experience at Tableau and their current hypergrowth startup, followed by a fireside chat with Madrona's Mark Nelson on what separates companies that scale from those stuck in prototype limbo. The whole thing wraps with plenty of time to pick their brains directly.

events.title: Seattle Consumer AI Founders Meetup (B2C)
events.description: Seattle's B2C AI founder scene is converging for an unfiltered roundtable where early-stage to growth-stage builders will swap real talk on what's actually working in consumer AI right now. Expect genuine conversation with founders shipping products people use daily—no corporate theater, just people who get it.

events.title: Spark + AI: Ignite the Future
events.description:
# Summary

A tech meetup will feature expert speakers diving into industry insights, with registration through Luma required to secure your spot.

events.title: AI for Good & Just Transition Challenge
events.description: Participants with a knack for innovation and problem-solving will tackle community issues using AI, receive expert mentoring, and compete for prizes during challenges on July 25.

events.title: GraphCon
events.description: GraphCon will bring together builders, architects, and data scientists for an intimate day diving into cutting-edge graph technology, featuring hands-on sessions on GraphRAG, production scaling, and a live reverse hackathon where attendees pitch real problems for expert solutions moderated by architect Vaibhav Gupta. Expect high-signal discussions that prioritize peer-to-peer knowledge sharing alongside industry insights from leaders across the graph stack.

events.title: Junior NATEA x UW Robotics, CS, & AI Summer Program
events.description: Middle schoolers will dive into cutting-edge robotics and AI with instructors who are actual UW professors and world-class robotics champions, all using the newest LEGO® CS & AI technology.

events.title: Lawyers Who Build: The Future of Legal Tech & AI | Batesly x Clearbrief
events.description: Two former litigators building AI tools for the legal profession—Rachel Bender (Batesly) and Jacqueline Schafer (Clearbrief, 2025 Legalweek Innovator of the Year)—will dig into where AI actually helps lawyers versus where it creates risk, what it takes to earn the trust of a skeptical profession, and what legal tech will actually look like over the next few years.

events.title: Foundation Models Go Vertical | Ascend
events.description: Founders betting their companies on foundation models will get grilled by the legal minds and competitors actually shaping the landscape—expect real talk about whether labs will actually compete with their own developers.

events.title: AI & SaaS growth: Drinks in the sun
events.description: Founders and product leaders focused on AI and SaaS products will dive into strategies for building, growing, and monetizing their innovations. Expect insights tailored to professionals shaping the future of tech-driven solutions.

events.title: AI Startup Secret Sauce
events.description: Founders and investors will dive into what actually makes AI startups succeed, with industry veterans unpacking the strategies and insights that separate winners from the rest.

events.title: SaaS & AI Founder + Investor Rooftop Happy Hour | NachoNacho
events.description: SaaStr San Francisco will bring together Seed+ founders, C-suite executives, VCs, and tech media for an exclusive rooftop happy hour where genuine connections happen over sponsored drinks and appetizers—perfect for founders to pitch their vision and investors to scout their next big opportunity.

events.title: STW Startup Showcase at The Showbox (Live Demos) | J.P. Morgan
events.description: Seattle's startup scene will bring its A-game with 23% of the country's AI engineering talent on display at this showcase, where early-stage founders will pitch alongside recently unicorn-status companies, backed by J.P. Morgan and a powerhouse lineup of local VCs like Madrona and Ascend. Expect cutting-edge product demos, investor reverse pitches, and an 80s/90s rock cover band keeping the energy high throughout the evening.

events.title: HackerX AI (Employer Registration)
events.description: Over 100 AI professionals will converge for an evening of networking and peer exchange, bringing together expertise across machine learning, AI research, and industry applications.

events.title: Stripe Developer Meetup Seattle July 2026
events.description: Stripe's inaugural Seattle developer meetup will bring together builders working with Stripe for an evening of product insights, best practices, and talks from Stripe engineers and industry experts like those from Auth0, covering topics from AI wallet agents to secure integrations.

events.title: Founder panel: AI & SaaS product-led growth | Tech Week 2026
events.description: Founders and growth leaders will dig into the full PLG playbook—from initial customer acquisition and conversion tactics to expansion and retention strategies—with experienced founders breaking down what actually works.

events.title: Huge Annual Summer BBQ | AI House
events.description: Over 700 AI founders, engineers, investors, and researchers will converge for Seattle Tech Week's flagship celebration, complete with a science fair showcasing the region's hottest startups alongside music, drinks, and plenty of unexpected connections that'll shape what comes next in the industry.

events.title: Casino Night for Founders, Builders, and Funders | MongoDB
events.description: Seattle's top AI founders, builders, and funders will gather for a casino-themed networking night featuring poker, blackjack, roulette, and more, with industry heavyweights like MongoDB, NVIDIA, Madrona Ventures, and Temporal hosting the action. Top chip earners will compete for prizes including an NVIDIA RTX 4060, Apple AirPods Max, and Meta Smart Glasses.

events.title: Understanding AI
events.description: A free, public workshop will demystify AI's presence in everyday life and work, making it accessible to everyone without requiring a technical background.

events.title: What's Next for AI in Washington? Reflections on the AI Task Force Report
events.description: Washington State's AI Task Force will discuss their report findings and chart the path forward for AI policy and implementation across the state. This public conversation will bring together key stakeholders and policymakers to explore the implications and next steps for AI governance in the region.

events.title: Camp AI: Agents at Work
events.description: Tech founders and AI builders will gather for live demos and a panel discussion on shipping agentic workflows at scale, with plenty of time to network with rising startups and leaders pushing AI innovation forward. Expect real conversations about developing and scaling AI agents, backed by industry voices diving into what actually works.

events.title: AI House Founder Mental Health Affinity Group Meetup - Anxiety in the Age of AI Catastrophe
events.description: Tech product founders will explore managing anxiety and high-stakes decision-making amid AI pressures, guided by executive coach Brett Greene, PCC, in an intimate peer support setting focused on emotional resilience and shared experiences.

events.title: New Tech August 2026 Meetup
events.description: New Tech Seattle’s August meetup will gather top developers, founders, and industry leaders for brief, insightful presentations on cutting-edge tech like AI, blockchain, and green tech, along with valuable networking and career-building opportunities in a welcoming community of over 60,000 professionals.

events.title: AI Agents in the Wild: Building, Scaling & Deploying in Production
events.description: Top minds from GitHub, Salesforce, and AWS will explore how they're taking AI agents from demos straight into real-world production at scale.

events.title: Pacific Northwest Software Symposium
events.description: The Pacific Northwest Software Symposium will bring together developers of all levels to explore cutting-edge topics like AI, Machine Learning, Spring 6, DevOps, and Microservices through hands-on sessions led by industry experts. Whether you're a senior engineer or just growing your skills, you'll get the chance to level up your software development game across architecture, modern Java, and big data.

events.title: AI House Founder Mental Health Affinity Group Meetup - Imposter Syndrome in Leadership
events.description: Tech product founders will explore imposter syndrome’s impact on leadership with insights from executive coach Brett Greene, PCC, in a confidential peer circle focused on resilience, authentic connection, and practical strategies to manage self-doubt while leading effectively.

events.title: New Tech September 2026 Meetup
events.description: The upcoming New Tech Seattle meetup will bring together a vibrant mix of developers, founders, executives, and innovators for a night of rapid-fire presentations, insightful Q&A, and relaxed networking with tasty bites and drinks. Attendees will gain fresh perspectives on AI, cybersecurity, blockchain, and more, connecting within a community fueled by career-driven professionals and industry leaders.

events.title: AI House Founder Mental Health Affinity Group Meetup - Relationships Under Founder Pressure
events.description: Tech product founders will explore the emotional toll of startup life on personal relationships in a confidential peer-led discussion facilitated by Brett Greene, an executive coach and mental health advocate. This intimate session offers a safe space to share struggles and gain support from fellow founders navigating similar pressures.

events.title: New Tech October 2026 Meetup - AI Week Preview
events.description: New Tech Seattle’s October meetup will bring together over 60,000 tech pros—from founders to developers and executives—for rapid-fire presentations on cutting-edge tech like AI, blockchain, and sustainability, alongside casual networking with industry leaders, recruiters, and innovators.

events.title: AI House Founder Mental Health Affinity Group Meetup - The Addiction Patterns of High-Performers
events.description: Tech product founders will explore the addictive patterns tied to high-performance culture, from stimulant reliance to biohacking extremes, in a confidential peer-led conversation facilitated by executive coach Brett Greene, PCC. This intimate meetup offers a rare space for founders to openly reflect on mental health challenges and share insights without pressure or judgment.

events.title: New Tech November 2026 Meetup
events.description: New Tech Seattle's November meetup will bring together over 60,000 Northwest tech professionals including developers, founders, executives, and investors for insightful 8-minute presentations by industry leaders, lively networking, and a look into cutting-edge fields like AI, cybersecurity, and blockchain—all with tasty bites, drinks, and a laid-back neighborhood vibe.

events.title: AI House Founder Mental Health Affinity Group Meetup - Redefining Success Beyond Exit or Acquisition
events.description: Tech product founders in AI and tech will engage in a confidential, peer-led conversation facilitated by executive coach Brett Greene, PCC, focusing on redefining success beyond financial exits, aligning values, and fostering sustainable leadership without burnout.

events.title: New Tech December 2026 Meetup
events.description: New Tech Seattle’s December meetup will bring together over 60,000 tech professionals—including developers, founders, executives, and investors—for rapid-fire presentations by industry leaders and vibrant neighborhood-style networking fueled by career-focused insights and innovations across AI, cybersecurity, fintech, and more. Attendees will enjoy a friendly atmosphere with light bites and drinks while connecting with highly qualified peers and community resources.
